TRIM47-facilitated PLK1 stabilization promotes the proliferation of liver cancer cells

evidence_entities.text: TRIM47 was significantly elevated in HCC tissues and positively correlated with advanced clinical stage and poor overall survival
TRIM47 knockdown suppressed LC cell growth, while its overexpression promoted tumor proliferation
TRIM47 catalyzes K63-linked ubiquitination of polo-like kinase 1 (PLK1), leading to its stabilization
TRIM47 facilitates cell cycle progression by alleviating G2/M phase arrest and inhibits apoptosis in LC cells
pharmacological inhibition of PLK1 effectively abrogated TRIM47-driven tumor growth in xenograft models
